title: TAMARACK Safe Shell Snippets
description: Reference-only shell helpers for local review, copying, and optional export into ~/.zshrc.

header: # Review before use. Generated from TAMARACK knowledge base.
# Dangerous snippets remain reference-only and are omitted from this export.

safetyRules: Do not paste large blocks directly into a live terminal session.
Edit ~/.zshrc intentionally with nano ~/.zshrc or another editor you trust.
Review every alias and function before installing it.
Avoid overriding destructive commands unless the snippet is clearly marked and justified.
Aliases that wrap rm or other destructive commands should be treated as caution or dangerous.
Snippets stay reference material until you explicitly copy them into ~/.zshrc yourself.

snippets.title: Long listing
snippets.description: Show a human-readable long listing for the current directory.
snippets.notes: Good default when you want file sizes without hidden files.

snippets.title: Show hidden files
snippets.description: List all files, including dotfiles, in long format.
snippets.notes: Useful when inspecting project config and hidden files.

snippets.title: All files with sizes
snippets.description: List all files in long format with human-readable sizes.
snippets.notes: Pairs hidden-file visibility with readable file sizes.

snippets.title: Oldest-first detailed view
snippets.description: List files with details, hidden entries, and oldest items near the top.
snippets.notes: Useful when you want to see the newest files accumulate at the bottom.

snippets.title: Newest-first detailed view
snippets.description: List files with details and put recent entries near the top.
snippets.notes: Helpful for quickly spotting the most recently changed files.

snippets.title: Git status
snippets.description: Show the current repository status.
snippets.notes: Safe read-only shortcut for checking branch and file state.

snippets.title: Git add
snippets.description: Stage files intentionally with git add.
snippets.notes: Keeps the original git add behavior but shorter to type.

snippets.title: Stage everything
snippets.description: Stage tracked and untracked changes across the repository.
snippets.notes: Review git status first so you do not stage unrelated work by accident.

snippets.title: Git commit
snippets.description: Run git commit with the normal interactive message flow.
snippets.notes: Convenient shorthand when you want the full commit experience.

snippets.title: Git commit with message
snippets.description: Commit with an inline message flag ready to fill.
snippets.notes: Still review the staged diff before using a one-line commit shortcut.

snippets.title: Git push
snippets.description: Push the current branch to its configured remote.
snippets.notes: Pushing affects a shared remote, so make sure the branch and remote are right.

snippets.title: Compact decorated log
snippets.description: Show a short graph view of the latest commits across branches.
snippets.notes: Read-only shortcut for a quick branch history snapshot.

snippets.title: Git diff
snippets.description: Show unstaged changes in the current repository.
snippets.notes: Useful before staging or committing so you know exactly what changed.

snippets.title: Git checkout
snippets.description: Use git checkout with the original broad behavior.
snippets.notes: Checkout can switch branches or restore paths, so confirm what arguments you pass.

snippets.title: Git branch
snippets.description: List or manage branches with a shorter command.
snippets.notes: Mostly read-only in day-to-day use unless you pass delete or rename flags.

snippets.title: SSH to Blackbird
snippets.description: Use the saved blackbird SSH host alias with a short command.
snippets.notes: Requires a matching Host blackbird entry in ~/.ssh/config before it will work.

snippets.title: Short SSH to Blackbird
snippets.description: Even shorter alias for the same blackbird SSH host entry.
snippets.notes: Keep the alias only if blackbird is a stable host name in your SSH config.

snippets.title: Python 3 shortcut
snippets.description: Prefer python3 explicitly with a short alias.
snippets.notes: Useful on systems where plain python is absent or points somewhere unexpected.

snippets.title: Serve current directory
snippets.description: Start a simple Python static file server on port 8000.
snippets.notes: This exposes the current directory over HTTP. Prefer a localhost-only bind if you only need private local access.

snippets.title: npm install
snippets.description: Shorthand for npm install.
snippets.notes: Useful for projects that stick with npm rather than pnpm or yarn.

snippets.title: npm run
snippets.description: Shorter prefix for package scripts.
snippets.notes: Pairs well with scripts like dev, build, and test that follow next.

snippets.title: npm run dev
snippets.description: Run the local development script with one word.
snippets.notes: Only keep this if dev is a common package script across your active projects.

snippets.title: npm run build
snippets.description: Run the package build script quickly.
snippets.notes: Convenient when a project consistently uses npm run build.

snippets.title: npm test
snippets.description: Run the package test script with a short alias.
snippets.notes: A short alias is handy, but remember some repos use test for watch or integration jobs.

snippets.title: Edit zsh config
snippets.description: Open your zsh config directly in nano.
snippets.notes: Safe enough for intentional edits, but changes to ~/.zshrc can affect every future shell.

snippets.title: Reload zsh config
snippets.description: Reload the current shell configuration without opening a new terminal.
snippets.notes: Run zsh -n ~/.zshrc first if you changed functions or blocks and want a safer syntax check.

snippets.title: Make and enter directory
snippets.description: Create a directory path if needed and enter it immediately.
snippets.notes: Keep this as a function rather than an alias so it can change the current shell directory.
